---
page-layout: full
pagetitle: "Ian Phillips | Philosophy and Psychological & Brain Sciences"
description: "Ian Phillips is Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Philosophy and Psychological & Brain Sciences at Johns Hopkins University."
---

::: {.home-header}

![](images/ian-phillips-web.jpg){.portrait}

# Ian Phillips

::: {.subtitle}
Bloomberg Distinguished Professor of Philosophy and Psychological & Brain Sciences  
Johns Hopkins University
:::

::: {.profile-links}
[![](images/icons/google-scholar.svg){.profile-icon} Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.co.uk/citations?user=2fmTJzkAAAAJ&hl=en) [![](images/icons/researchgate.svg){.profile-icon} ResearchGate](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Ian-Phillips-6) [![](images/icons/bluesky.svg){.profile-icon} Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/ianbphillips.bsky.social) [CV](cv.qmd)
:::

::: {.mobile-home-nav}
[Papers](papers.qmd) · [CV](cv.qmd) · [Contact](contact.qmd)
:::

:::

::: {.home-main}

::: {.subtle-links}

I am jointly appointed in the William H. Miller III [Department of Philosophy](https://philosophy.jhu.edu/) and the [Department of Psychological and Brain Sciences](https://pbs.jhu.edu/). I am also a regular visiting professor at [Oxford University](https://www.ox.ac.uk/).

My work lies at the intersection of philosophy and the mind/brain sciences, with particular interests in perception, memory, imagination, consciousness, and temporal experience. I am also writing a biography of [Gareth Evans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gareth_Evans_(philosopher)).

:::
## Recent updates

::: {.recent-updates}

- “[Response duration tracks confidence](files/papers/response-duration.pdf),” in press at *PNAS*.
- “[Pretending not to know reveals a capacity for model-based self-simulation](files/papers/pretending-not-to-know.pdf),” published in *Psychological Science*.
- “[Event-based warping: A relative distortion of time within events](files/papers/event-based-warping.pdf),” published in *Journal of Experimental Psychology: General*.
- “[Aphantasia reimagined](files/papers/aphantasia-reimagined.pdf),” published in *Noûs*.

:::

:::
